heroHeadline: Gang Attacks Kill 8+ in SE Haiti; Jacmel Protests; Culture Ministry Fires Officials Over Citadelle Stampede; US House Passes TPS Extension
heroSubtitle: Gang attacks attributed to Viv Ansanm-affiliated groups killed at least 7–8 people in Seguin (Marigot commune, Sud-Est) on April 13–14, 2026, displacing 4,000+ and burning the local police station — the first large-scale gang-driven displacement recorded in the Sud-Est department; protests shut down Jacmel on April 15–16. Nine people including 5 police officers were arrested and the Haiti Culture Ministry fired officials over the April 11 Citadelle Laferrière stampede (25 confirmed dead, revised from initial 30+), with three days of national mourning declared April 14–16. The UN reported on April 17 that only ~20% of the $880M 2026 HRP is funded; 5.8M Haitians face food crisis. The US House passed bipartisan legislation on April 18 requiring a three-year TPS extension for Haiti. Haiti and the Dominican Republic announced plans to reopen direct air links in May.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN BINUH, OHCHR, OCHA, IOM, Reuters, AP, ABC News, Miami Herald, Al Jazeera, France 24, Le Nouvelliste, ACLED, ICG, HRW, WFP, Haitian Times, Jamaica Observer, RNDDH, PassBlue, Amnesty International, IMF, HaitiLibre, JURIST, International Rescue Committee
